label: Hybrid retrieval & reranking

node.label: Hybrid retrieval & reranking
node.definition: Hybrid retrieval fuses lexical and dense candidates, then reranks them with stronger cross-encoders, listwise models, or task-specific scorers.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Hybrid retrieval & reranking is modeled as depending on Sparse lexical retrieval, Dense vector retrieval and feeding downstream nodes such as Retrieval-augmented generation, Vector-search and retrieval services, Neural search, RAG & question answering.
examples: atlas downstream link to Retrieval-augmented generation
atlas downstream link to Vector-search and retrieval services
atlas downstream link to Neural search, RAG & question answering
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Sparse lexical retrieval, Dense vector retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: Hybrid retrieval & reranking stores, selects, or obtains information through Sparse lexical retrieval.
Hybrid retrieval & reranking stores, selects, or obtains information through Dense vector retrieval.

In this atlas graph, Hybrid retrieval & reranking has a `uses` dependency on Margin, ranking & metric objectives because Margin, ranking & metric objectives is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
